description_long: This arcade game cabinet features a tall, rectangular structure with a slightly angled front face, typical of classic arcade machines. The front panel includes a large display screen labeled 'ARCADIA 2000' at the top, with a 'GAME OVER' message visible on the screen. Below the screen is a control panel equipped with a single red joystick and several colored buttons, arranged for single-player use. The lower section of the cabinet houses a coin slot and a vented panel, suggesting space for internal electronics and coin storage. The cabinet is predominantly dark blue with subtle yellow trim outlining its edges, and the side panels are plain with no visible artwork or decals. The overall design is robust and functional, suitable for placement in arcades, entertainment venues, or urban recreational spaces.
description: A tall, dark blue arcade game cabinet with a large screen, single joystick, colored buttons, and a coin slot, accented by yellow trim and a marquee labeled 'ARCADIA 2000'. The side panels are plain and unadorned.
description_view_0: Front view showing the screen with 'ARCADIA 2000' marquee, control panel with joystick and buttons, and a coin slot below.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the cabinet's depth, angled front, and plain blue side panel with yellow trim.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat, featureless back panel with yellow trim outlining the edges.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the angled front and plain blue side panel with yellow trim.
attribute_screen_type: CRT display, large and flat with a visible bezel, typical of classic arcade cabinets.
attribute_control_panel_layout: Single-player layout with one red joystick on the left and four colored buttons arranged in a row to the right; control panel is dark with colored button accents.
attribute_marquee_artwork: Present; features the text 'ARCADIA 2000' in a retro, gradient style with a dark background and subtle color highlights, evoking a classic arcade theme.
